behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with a real git repo and real rename detection output (rename heuristics/status codes). A mocked git layer can miss real rename formatting/threshold behavior and falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, then rename it (and optionally edit it). Run the real CLI and assert the stdout report shows the file only under `renamed`, with no corresponding increment in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git diff behavior for binary files and real subprocess/stdout handling. In-process fakes usually do not reproduce exact binary diff markers and edge-case output shapes that cause crashes.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a committed binary file change (e.g., image bytes), run the CLI, and assert exit code is success, no crash occurs, and binary changes are reflected in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The real failure occurs at git command/subprocess boundary (`HEAD` absent, non-zero exits, stderr text), which is OS/process behavior not reliably captured by mocks alone.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert successful execution with an empty structured report on stdout instead of an error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness check across real repository history, git command output, and CLI aggregation/output formatting. Mocking any of those can hide true mismatches from real git behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history containing known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI against that repo, and assert each category count matches the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics and CLI interaction with actual git state. Stubs rarely capture nuanced staged/unstaged behavior and can mask regressions.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, produce equivalent file changes once as unstaged and once staged (or compare two controlled runs), execute the real CLI in both states, and assert identical structured stdout output.
